title: Delicate Gooey Ukrainian Noodle Dish
description: A peppery and gooey Ukrainian noodle dish featuring sansho, perfect for a monsoon day, and Buddhist temple friendly as it is vegetarian.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the egg noodles according to the package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and add the sliced mushrooms, cooking until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Sprinkle the ground sansho pepper and paprika over the mushroom mixture and stir to combine.
In a small bowl, whisk together the flour and a splash of the vegetable broth until smooth, then add the mixture to the skillet, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the remaining vegetable broth to the skillet, whisking continuously to avoid lumps, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the sauce simmer for a few minutes until it thickens, stirring occasionally.
Drain the cooked noodles and add them to the skillet, tossing to combine with the sauce and mushroom mixture.
Season the dish with salt to taste, then serve hot.
